condition_derivations.proof: If PO1 is stated over D1RestrictionSystem pairs, richer validity is part of the domain of discourse.
If PO1 is stated over D1RestrictionSystem pairs, restricted validity is part of the domain of discourse.
A finite projection cannot be evaluated unless every source site has a target site.

condition_derivations.independence_witness: no feasible D1RestrictionSystem case with AC6=True and AC4=False
condition_derivations.proof: T26 defines obstruction_detected as local_satisfiable and not global_exists, so AC6 entails AC4.
A restriction system can show profile loss, but named forgotten structure is external ProjectionCase evidence.
The other conditions can hold while the restricted system has a global section.
The other conditions can hold while the richer system is already obstructed.

dependency_edges.reason: A PO1 candidate must first have a well-formed richer D1RestrictionSystem.
A PO1 candidate must also have a well-formed restricted D1RestrictionSystem.
Projection is finite and definable exactly when the site map is total.
The restricted target must have a finite gluing obstruction.
In T26, obstruction_detected means local patches are satisfiable and no global assignment exists.
The richer source must have a global section, so the obstruction is not inherited.
Forgotten named structure is stored on ProjectionCase, not inside D1RestrictionSystem alone.

recommendation: Replace the independent seven-condition posture with a four-principle basis: typed pair, definable projection, projection-created nontrivial obstruction, and informative forgotten structure. Keep the AC1-AC7 checklist as an expanded audit surface, but fold AC4 into AC6 in formal statements. Retain AC5 as an explicit non-intrinsic admissibility guard until forgotten structure is made first-class inside the formal object.

removal_audits.conclusion: Without AC1, remove_ac1_case admits an invalid typed object as PO1 evidence.
Without AC2, remove_ac2_case admits an invalid typed object as PO1 evidence.
Without AC3, remove_ac3_case admits a category-change boundary as a projection.
Removing AC4 changes no classification because AC6 already implies local satisfiability in the executable definition.
Without AC5, remove_ac5_case admits a lossless or unnamed projection as PO1 evidence.
Without AC6, remove_ac6_case admits a projection where the restricted system is not obstructed.
Without AC7, remove_ac7_case admits a shared obstruction as projection-created.

structural_principles.boundary: This is a typing obligation, not a discovered empirical rule.
structural_principles.name: Typed restriction-system pair
structural_principles.statement: Both source and target must satisfy the T26 finite restriction-system axioms.

structural_principles.boundary: Category-change cases fail here rather than becoming negative PO1 examples.
structural_principles.name: Definable finite projection
structural_principles.statement: A projection must have a total site map into the restricted system.

structural_principles.boundary: AC4 is redundant once AC6 uses T26 obstruction_detected.
structural_principles.name: Projection-created nontrivial obstruction
structural_principles.statement: The restricted system is obstructed and the richer system is not.

structural_principles.boundary: The named-forgotten-structure half lives on ProjectionCase metadata.
structural_principles.name: Informative forgotten structure
structural_principles.statement: The projection must forget named structure and measurably lose profile data.

theorem_verdict: AC1-AC7 are not seven independent empirical rules. AC4 derives from AC6; AC1 and AC2 are type obligations; AC3, AC6, and AC7 are structural projection/gluing obligations. AC5 remains an extra informative-loss condition not derivable from D1RestrictionSystem alone.
